name: cursor-dev
description: Authoring kit for Cursor (the AI editor) extensions, current as of June 2026, Cursor 3.7. Owns the Cursor-specific half of extension development: plugins (.cursor-plugin/plugin.json manifest, marketplace.json multi-plugin repos, cursor.com/marketplace publishing with its open-source requirement, Team Marketplaces from private GitHub repos), rules (.cursor/rules/*.mdc only — plain .md is silently ignored — with the four application types and Team > Project > User precedence), skills (agentskills.io standard with Cursor's extra discovery paths including legacy .claude/skills/ and .codex/skills/, the paths glob-visibility key, disable-model-invocation, and the commands-to-skills migration), hooks (~22 camelCase events in hooks.json with version field, prompt-type LLM hooks, permission allow/deny/ask protocol — deliberately NOT Claude Code's schema), subagents (.cursor/agents/ plus .claude/.codex compat reads, no tools field), and MCP (mcp.json transports, OAuth, ${env:NAME} interpolation, install deeplinks, permissions.json). Ships a deterministic lane: validate-cursor-artifact.sh checks a Cursor-bound artifact directory for manifest validity, .mdc frontmatter parse and rule-type combos, ignored plain-.md rules, skill name/directory mismatches, and unknown hook event names, on the shared JSON finding contract.
